issues: 1x silhouette is a mushy indistinct blob — unreadable as a key
poor contrast on bright/light background — gold washes into cream
low charm factor — ornate but not toy-soft; gem loop reads as mechanical rather than friendly
no rounded-softness language in the bow shape; bits feel sharp and angular at pixel level
reasoning: At 1x the sprite fails the silhouette test immediately: it collapses to a vertical smudge with no legible key identity. The 4x panels show decent pixel-art craft — gold/orange palette, oval gem hub, decorative bow — and on dark backgrounds the warm gold reads reasonably well. However on the bright/light panel (rightmost) contrast drops sharply and the key nearly disappears against the pale cream, meaning it fails the dual-background requirement. Charm potential exists in the gem-and-bow motif but the shapes trend angular and mechanical rather than round-soft-toy; compared to Echoes of Wisdom's plump friendly silhouettes this reads as 'dungeon prop' not 'adorable artifact'. Charming-but-mushy rule applies: the 1x collapse alone disqualifies a pass.
